transport: plain HTTP/1.1 with browser user agent
summary: AJ Madison remains a useful appliance target but is not promotable for Reno Goat. Direct product, category, search, sitemap, and visible catadmin API routes all returned PerimeterX/CAPTCHA or equivalent blocked responses. Public static assets reveal Algolia client code and AJ Madison catadmin/search endpoint shapes, but no replayable product-price rows were proven.
product_gap: exact appliance model rows with prices, including JOESC330RM
AJ Madison appliance search rows suitable for active fan-out

static_routes_tested.result: HTTP 200; lists sitemapindex.xml and api/sitemap.xml; disallows /papi/*

asset_findings: 404 script guesses returned the AJ Madison HTML shell, not JavaScript, but the shell exposed public asset URLs, category links, an Algolia experiment flag, and public frontend keys.
Downloaded public assets common.d0cc10ca.js, prelude.c969bef3.js, and legacy.b8d2f30c.js.

Assets expose Algolia client code and endpoint strings including /papi/search/search-multi-query.json.php, /papi/search/search-events.json.php, /papi/list-page/top-searches.json.php, /webservices/api/catadmin/brands/, /webservices/api/catadmin/categories/, and /webservices/api/catadmin/products/.
No literal Algolia app ID plus search key pair was found in the static JavaScript assets.
/papi/* is robots-disallowed and was not selected as a Reno Goat source path.
next_action: Treat AJ Madison as a GHST-293/browser-resource extraction candidate. Do not promote until exact-model or category appliance rows with prices replay from the CLI without relying on search-result snippets.
